pi_items.source: Task description: 'Enter 120 in the RMS Voltage field.' Confirmed in setup_task.sh and verifier.py

Mathematical calculation: Peak = RMS × √2 = 120 × 1.41421356... = 169.7056V, which rounds to 169.7. Verified against electrical engineering standards and web references (https://www.allaboutcircuits.com/tools/rms-voltage-calculator/, https://voltagecalculator.online/tools/rms-to-peak-converter.php?lang=en)
Verifier.py line 31: metadata.get('tolerance', 1.5). Used in math.isclose(val, expected_peak, abs_tol=tolerance) for accepting values within ±1.5V of expected peak
Task description: 'Write the resulting Peak Voltage value...to a text file at /sdcard/tasks/peak_voltage.txt'. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 8 and verifier.py
Task description: 'Save a screenshot of the result screen to /sdcard/tasks/rms_to_peak_result.png'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task converts 120V RMS to peak voltage using the formula Peak = RMS × √2. The correct peak voltage is 169.7V (more precisely 169.7056V). The task requires saving the result to /sdcard/tasks/peak_voltage.txt and a screenshot to /sdcard/tasks/rms_to_peak_result.png. A tolerance of ±1.5V is used for verification, accepting values in the range 168.2V to 171.2V. All metadata values are mathematically correct and consistent with the task description and verification scripts.
